market_context: Markets are experiencing cautious optimism despite ongoing geopolitical tensions. The S&P 500 has erased war-related losses amid hopes for renewed US-Iran peace talks, while oil prices have pulled back from $100+ highs on diplomatic prospects. Bitcoin and Ethereum are surging on short squeezes and institutional flows, while bank earnings are mixed with JPMorgan beating expectations but Wells Fargo missing.

predictions.reasoning: Strong momentum from Monday's 1.02% S&P 500 gain and futures pointing higher on Iran peace talk optimism. Pre-market data shows continued diplomatic engagement which is reducing geopolitical risk premium. Tech sector strength and financial earnings beats (JPMorgan) provide fundamental support for further gains.

Wells Fargo reported Q1 EPS of $1.56 vs $1.60 estimate and revenue of $21.45B vs $21.97B estimate - both misses. The bank is underperforming peers this year (-8% YTD) and has been downgraded by analysts. Earnings disappointment likely triggers further selling pressure.

Bitcoin broke above $74K resistance triggering a massive short squeeze with $430M in liquidations. Renewed Iran peace talk hopes are boosting risk assets, while institutional demand remains strong with ETF activity. Technical momentum suggests continuation toward $75K-$76K resistance zone.

Oil prices have fallen from $100+ highs to mid-$90s as hopes for US-Iran diplomatic progress reduce supply disruption fears. WTI dropped 6% on talks of second round negotiations. Pakistan is reportedly arranging follow-up meetings, which would further ease energy shock concerns.

Despite JPMorgan beating Q1 estimates with record trading revenue ($11.6B), the stock fell 1% pre-market after lowering 2026 NII guidance from $104.5B to $103B. This guidance cut is concerning for future earnings potential, though strong trading results provide some offset to selling pressure.
